rules.title: Missing Independent Witness for Seizure
rules.description: Seizure of narcotic substances must be conducted in the presence of independent witnesses as per NDPS Act requirements

remediation.summary: Ensure seizure was conducted with independent witnesses and document their details
steps: Verify at least two independent witnesses were present during seizure
Record full particulars of each panch witness (name, address, contact)
Obtain signatures of panch witnesses on the seizure panchnama
If independent witnesses were unavailable, document reasons per Section 100(5) CrPC/BNSS 105

rules.title: Missing Sample Sealing Certificate
rules.description: Seized narcotic substance samples must be properly sealed and a sealing certificate must be referenced in the chargesheet

remediation.summary: Document proper sealing of seized substance samples
steps: Ensure seized substance is divided into sample and bulk quantities
Seal both sample and bulk portions separately with unique seal impressions
Record seal details (seal number/impression) in the seizure panchnama
Obtain sealing certificate signed by the IO and panch witnesses
Reference the sealing certificate in the chargesheet evidence schedule

rules.title: Missing Weighment Memo
rules.description: Seized narcotic substance must be weighed at the spot and a weighment memo prepared, as quantity determines the severity of the offence under NDPS Act

remediation.summary: Prepare and reference the weighment memo for seized substance
steps: Weigh the seized substance at the spot in presence of panch witnesses
Record both gross weight (with packing) and net weight (substance only)
Prepare a weighment memo signed by the IO and panch witnesses
Classify the quantity as small/commercial per NDPS Act notification
Reference the weighment memo in the chargesheet evidence schedule

rules.title: Missing Section 50 NDPS Compliance Notice
rules.description: If the accused was searched, mandatory compliance with Section 50 of NDPS Act must be documented - the accused must be informed of their right to be searched before a gazetted officer or a magistrate

steps: Inform the accused in writing of their right to be searched before a gazetted officer or magistrate
Record the accused's response (whether they opted for search before gazetted officer/magistrate or waived the right)
If accused opts for gazetted officer/magistrate search, comply before proceeding
Include the Section 50 notice and accused's response in the chargesheet evidence schedule
Non-compliance with Section 50 renders the trial vitiated - ensure strict compliance

rules.title: Missing FSL Chemical Analysis Report
rules.description: Chargesheet must reference the FSL chemical analysis report confirming the identity of the seized narcotic substance

remediation.summary: Include FSL chemical analysis report reference
steps: Send sealed sample to FSL for chemical analysis promptly
Reference FSL report number, date, and findings in the chargesheet
Confirm the substance identified by FSL matches the NDPS Act schedule
If FSL report is pending at the time of filing, note dispatch date and request number

rules.title: Missing Section 42/43 NDPS Compliance
rules.description: For searches conducted without warrant, compliance with Section 42 (prior information recording) or Section 43 (public place seizure) of NDPS Act must be documented

remediation.summary: Document compliance with Section 42 or Section 43 NDPS Act
steps: For Section 42: Record prior information in writing before search
Send copy of recorded information to immediate superior within 72 hours
For Section 43: Document the circumstances of seizure in a public place
Reference the empowered officer's authorization if applicable
Include all compliance documents in the chargesheet evidence schedule

rules.title: Missing Quantity Classification
rules.description: Chargesheet must specify whether the seized quantity falls under small quantity, intermediate quantity, or commercial quantity as per NDPS Act notifications

remediation.summary: Classify the seized quantity per NDPS Act thresholds
steps: Determine the substance type from the FSL report
Refer to the latest NDPS notification for quantity thresholds
Classify as small quantity (below threshold), intermediate, or commercial quantity (above threshold)
State the classification clearly in the chargesheet as it determines bail provisions and sentencing
